title: Traoré Declares Burkina Faso Should 'Forget About Democracy,' Extends Junta Rule to 2029
detail: Burkina Faso's junta leader Captain Ibrahim Traoré publicly declared that citizens should 'forget about democracy,' announcing an extension of his transitional government's rule to 2029 — five years beyond the original promised timeline. All political parties had already been banned in January 2026. Traoré, who holds the AES rotating chairmanship, framed the rejection of democratic norms as a security and sovereignty imperative. The move drew immediate condemnation from Western governments and international democracy advocates. Under Traoré, Burkina Faso has expelled French forces, invited Russia's Africa Corps, and suspended all civil society organizations critical of the junta. The 2029 target date is widely seen as indefinite, given Traoré's prior pattern of unilaterally extending transition timelines. The democracy rejection comes amid one of the worst security crises in Burkina Faso's history, with JNIM and IS-Sahel controlling an estimated 50–60% of national territory.

sources.name: Democracy Now! — 'Burkina Faso coup leader declares democracy isn't for us after extending rule to 2029'
The World / PRX — 'The future of Burkina Faso's democracy is in question'

media.caption: Burkina Faso coup leader declares 'democracy isn't for us' after extending rule to 2029
